content: A stone is projected at an angle $60^{\circ}$ to the horizontal. The ratio of kinetic energy of the stone at point of projection to its kinetic energy at highest point of flight will be $\left(\cos 60^{\circ}=0.5\right)$
explanation:
At projection:

$$ \text { speed }=u \Rightarrow K \cdot E_{\cdot 1}=\frac{1}{2} m u^2 $$

At highest point:

$$ \begin{aligned} & \text { speed }=u \cos \theta \\ \Rightarrow \quad & K E_2=\frac{1}{2} m(u \cos \theta)^2=\frac{1}{2} m\left(\frac{u}{2}\right)^2=\frac{1}{8} m u^2 \end{aligned} $$

Ratio:

$$ \frac{\mathrm{KE}_1}{\mathrm{KE}_2}=\frac{\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{mu}^2}{\frac{1}{8} \mathrm{mu}^2}=\frac{1 / 2}{1 / 8}=\frac{4}{1} $$

content: Two bodies with kinetic energies in the ratio $3: 1$ are moving with equal linear momentum. The ratio of their masses is
explanation:
We know,

Linear momentum and kinetic energy are related by $\mathrm{p}=\sqrt{2 \mathrm{mK}_{\mathrm{E}}}$

As linear momentum is

$$ \begin{array}{ll} & \sqrt{2 m_1 K E_1}=\sqrt{2 m_2 K E_2} \\ \therefore & \sqrt{2 m_1\left(3 K E_2\right)}=\sqrt{2 m_2 K E_2} \quad \ldots\left(K E_1=3 K E_2\right) \\ \therefore & \sqrt{3 m_1}=\sqrt{m_2} \\ \therefore & \frac{m_1}{m_2}=\frac{1}{3} \end{array} $$
